2605.26726.title: Measuring Prediction Uncertainty in Neural Cellular Automata
2605.26726.paper_summary: Neural cellular automata (NCA) provide a lightweight alternative to encoder-decoder segmentation networks. However, it can be difficult to decide when a prediction should be trusted. Here, we study uncertainty estimation for NCA-based medical image segmentation without modifying the underlying architecture or retraining the model. Our approach is motivated by viewing the NCA as a dynamical system where convergent attractors correspond to confident predictions. Concretely, we propose resilience, a simple measure that leverages the intrinsic iterative structure of NCAs by probing the stability of the final prediction under small perturbations of the automaton state. Predictions that return to the same solution are deemed confident, while those that change substantially are flagged as uncertain. We evaluate uncertainty by its ability to predict segmentation quality using selective prediction metrics ($Δ$Dice@90 and AURC) and ranking metrics (AUROC and AUPRC). Across multiple medical segmentation benchmarks, resilience identifies failure cases more reliably than baselines, improving trust and safety in NCA-based models.
2605.26726.paper_summary_zh: 神經細胞自動機（NCA）提供了一種輕量級的替代方案來取代編碼器-解碼器分割網絡。<lb/>然而，決定何時應該信任一個預測可能是困難的。<lb/>在這裡，我們研究了基於 NCA 的醫學影像分割的不確定性估計，而不修改底層架構或重新訓練模型。<lb/>我們的方法受到以下觀點的啟發：將 NCA 視為一個動態系統，其中收斂的吸引子對應於可信的預測。<lb/>具體而言，我們提出了韌性（resilience），這是一個簡單的度量，通過探測自動機狀態的小擾動下最終預測的穩定性，利用 NCA 的內在迭代結構。<lb/>返回相同解的預測被視為可信，而那些顯著改變的則被標記為不確定。<lb/>我們通過其預測分割質量的能力來評估不確定性，使用選擇性預測指標（$Δ$Dice@90 和 AURC）以及排名指標（AUROC 和 AUPRC）。<lb/>在多個醫學分割基準上，韌性比基準更可靠地識別失敗案例，提高了基於 NCA 的模型的信任度和安全性。
2605.26726.author: Ario Sadafi et.al.
2605.26726.authors: Ario Sadafi, Michael Deutges, Nassir Navab, Carsten Marr
